question_html:
Make sentences with the following group verbs:

look after, call off, carry out, set up, put off.
solution_html:
(a) She promised to look after my cat while I am away.
‎(b) They had to call off the picnic because of heavy rain.
‎(c) The scientist will carry out a new experiment next week.
‎(d) We plan to set up a community library in our area.
‎(e) The meeting was put off until next Monday.

Marks Distribution:
‎(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for making sentences with group verbs.

question_html:
Write down the correct tag questions of the following sentences:

(a) Nobody understood the instructions, _____

(b) Let us go for a walk, _____

(c) You used to play the guitar, _____

(d) She seldom visits her relatives, _____

(e) I am late, _____
solution_html:
(a) did they?

(b) shall we?

(c) didn’t you?

(d) does she?

(e) ain't I/aren't I?

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for writing the correct tag questions.

question_html: Write a paragraph on “Child Labour”.
solution_html:
Child Labour

Child labour is the employment of children in any form of work that deprives them of their childhood, education, and basic rights. It is a serious issue affecting millions of children globally, especially in developing countries. Children engaged in labour are often exposed to hazardous conditions, exploitation, and abuse. This practice hinders their physical, mental, and social development, as they are forced to work long hours for minimal or no pay. Poverty, lack of access to education, and societal norms are some of the root causes that perpetuate child labour. Although international laws, such as those from the International Labour Organization (ILO), aim to eliminate child labour, enforcement remains challenging in many regions. Addressing the issue requires a multi-faceted approach, including education, legal enforcement, and economic support for vulnerable families. Governments, non-governmental organizations, and communities must work together to create safer environments for children. Ending child labour is crucial for breaking cycles of poverty and ensuring a brighter future for the next generation.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for writing, the Title "Child Labour".

02 marks for explaining, how child labor causes harm to children and its bad impacts.

01 mark for writing, causes of the increase in child labour.

1.5 marks for writing, measures taken to end child labour.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

*0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.]
